**Luciano Lama** (14 October 1921 – 31 May 1996) was an Italian trade unionist and politician, General Secretary of [Italian General Confederation of Labour](/wiki/Italian_General_Confederation_of_Labour "Italian General Confederation of Labour") from 1970 to 1986.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Lama&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

### Role in the resistance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Lama&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Role in the resistance")]

Lama graduated in Political Sciences at the [University of Florence](/wiki/University_of_Florence "University of Florence") under the name of Boris Alberti, since he had to remain anonymous because he refused to join the [Republic of Salò](/wiki/Republic_of_Sal%C3%B2 "Republic of Salò").[[1]](#cite_note-1) When he was very young, Lama joined the [Italian Socialist Party](/wiki/Italian_Socialist_Party "Italian Socialist Party") and took part in the [resistance movement](/wiki/Italian_resistance_movement "Italian resistance movement"), contributing to free the city of [Forlì](/wiki/Forl%C3%AC "Forlì") from the [Nazis](/wiki/Nazi_Germany "Nazi Germany").[[2]](#cite_note-anpi-2)

### Deputy and secretary of the CGIL

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Lama&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Deputy and secretary of the CGIL")]

In 1946, Lama joined the [Italian Communist Party](/wiki/Italian_Communist_Party "Italian Communist Party"), with which he was elected to the [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Italian_Chamber_of_Deputies "Italian Chamber of Deputies") in 1958, in 1963 and in 1968. He left his seat when he joined the [Italian General Confederation of Labour](/wiki/Italian_General_Confederation_of_Labour "Italian General Confederation of Labour"), being elected Secretary-general of the [trade union](/wiki/List_of_trade_unions_in_Italy "List of trade unions in Italy") in 1970.[[2]](#cite_note-anpi-2)

On 17 February 1977, Lama was violently contested at the [University of Rome](/wiki/Sapienza_University_of_Rome "Sapienza University of Rome") by a group of young people, who adhered to extra-parliamentary positions.[[3]](#cite_note-mess-3)

In January 1978, in an assembly at the [EUR in Rome](/wiki/EUR,_Rome "EUR, Rome"), Lama proposed to the workers a politics of sacrifice, aimed at healing the Italian economy.[[4]](#cite_note-4) At the end of his secretary, in 1986, the [CGIL](/wiki/Italian_General_Confederation_of_Labour "Italian General Confederation of Labour") was strengthened in terms of political influence as it became the main point of reference for most of the employees.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Senator

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Lama&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Senator")]

In 1987, Lama was elected with the [Communist Party](/wiki/Italian_Communist_Party "Italian Communist Party") to the [Senate](/wiki/Italian_Senate "Italian Senate") and was re-elected in 1992 with the [Democratic Party of the Left](/wiki/Democratic_Party_of_the_Left "Democratic Party of the Left"). From 9 July 1987 to 14 April 1994, Lama was appointed Vice-president of the [Senate](/wiki/Italian_Senate "Italian Senate").[[2]](#cite_note-anpi-2)

### Mayor experience and death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luciano_Lama&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Mayor experience and death")]

In 1989, Lama was elected Mayor of [Amelia](/wiki/Amelia,_Umbria "Amelia, Umbria"), a town in the [province of Terni](/wiki/Province_of_Terni "Province of Terni"), and was re-elected in 1994, when he became the first Mayor elected directly by the people of [Amelia](/wiki/Amelia,_Umbria "Amelia, Umbria"), receiving support by the whole [Alliance of Progressives](/wiki/Alliance_of_Progressives "Alliance of Progressives").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Lama died in office on 31 May 1996, at the age of 74, after a long illness.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He is now buried in the [Verano Cemetery](/wiki/Campo_Verano "Campo Verano").[[3]](#cite_note-mess-3)
